title: Bias and Toxicity Auditing
concept: Auditing uses automated toxicity classifiers to scan model outputs for stereotypes, offensive language, or biased associations.

exercises.prompt: Why do developers audit AI models?

options.text: To find and fix toxic language and biases in AI outputs
To make the AI run on less electricity
To teach the AI how to play video games

exercises.explain: Auditing helps developers detect toxic behavior and stereotypes in AI models before they are used by real people.

cards.title: Keeping AI Fair and Clean
cards.body: AI models can read millions of web pages, but they also accidentally learn human prejudices. How do we scan through millions of AI answers to make sure they aren't repeating mean or unfair stereotypes? We use automated audit scanners!

cards.title: What is Auditing?
cards.body: Bias and toxicity auditing is the process of using automated scanner tools (called toxicity classifiers) to read through thousands of AI responses. These tools flag unfair stereotypes, hateful language, or mean associations so they can be fixed.

cards.title: The AI Health Inspector
cards.body: Imagine you are a restaurant health inspector. Instead of tasting every single meal yourself, you use a special scanner that instantly checks dishes for spoiled ingredients or germs. Auditing tools do the same thing for AI: they quickly scan thousands of generated sentences looking for "spoiled" words or unfair biases before they reach the customers.

cards.title: Spotting Stereotypes
cards.body: Suppose an AI is asked to write stories about different jobs. If we audit the results and find the AI only describes doctors as men and nurses as women, the audit flags this as a gender stereotype bias. Developers can then adjust the AI to write more balanced stories.

cards.title: Is AI Always Unbiased?
cards.body: Some people think AI is naturally fair because it's a computer. But since AI learns from human writing, it can easily repeat toxic or biased language. Regular audits are necessary to catch these hidden patterns.

cards.title: Before vs. After Auditing
cards.body: See how auditing changes what the user experiences by filtering out harmful bias.

left.text: The AI generates biased assumptions or offensive language directly to the user unchecked.

right.text: A classifier scans the output, flags toxic text, and prevents it from being displayed.

exercises.prompt: What is the main job of a toxicity classifier during an AI audit?

options.text: To scan text for stereotypes and offensive language
To make the AI write faster code
To translate the text into multiple languages
To create new images from text prompts

exercises.explain: Toxicity classifiers are trained to look for offensive language, hate speech, and stereotypes in text, making them perfect for automated audits.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Audits must always be done manually because computers cannot detect text bias.
exercises.explain: Audits are usually automated because AI models generate far too much text for human teams to review manually in a reasonable time.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of auditing tools.
exercises.sentence: Auditing systems use automated toxic text [blank] to scan AI outputs.
exercises.explain: Automated auditing relies on toxicity classifiers to scan through massive amounts of text and flag inappropriate language.

exercises.prompt: Which of these sentences is likely an audited AI output flagged for gender bias?

options.text: \"We need to hire the most qualified candidate for this engineering position.\"
\"Women are not suited for engineering roles because they are too emotional.\"
\"Our company has hired three new software engineers this month.\"

exercises.explain: The second option is an AI output because it displays a harmful stereotype associating career capability with gender, which an audit would flag.

exercises.prompt: How does automated bias and toxicity auditing help make AI safer for the public?

options.text: It manually rewrites every word the AI says by hand.
It uses automated classifiers to scan model outputs for stereotypes and toxic language.
It increases the speed of the AI's training process.
It deletes the AI model entirely if a single mistake is made.

exercises.explain: Auditing is an essential step before releasing an AI. By using toxicity classifiers to scan outputs, we can catch and fix offensive language and biased associations before the public interacts with the model.
